48.reason: [Variable 'i' is declared as a global variable and modified in loops; using it to compute 'num' after input may lead to incorrect count due to reuse in later loops. Also, 'i' starts at 1, so indexing mon[1..num] skips mon[0], but this isn't inherently wrong if intentional. However, the real issue is that the program uses global 'i' which gets reused in sorting and merging loops, causing logic errors.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Variable 'i' is declared as a global variable and modified in loops; using it to compute 'num' after input may lead to incorrect count due to reuse in later loops. reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Variable 'num' is declared after executable statements, which is invalid in C89/C90 standard. Declaration must come before any code in the block.]

69.reason: [After merging terms with same exponent, setting 'i = j' causes the outer loop to skip indices because the for-loop will then do i++, potentially skipping unprocessed terms. This leads to incorrect polynomial term combination.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [After merging terms with same exponent, setting 'i = j' causes the outer loop to skip indices because the for-loop will then do i++, potentially skipping unprocessed terms. This leads to incorrect polynomial term combination.]

51.reason: [Variable 'j' is redeclared inside the for loop. In C89/C90, variables must be declared at the beginning of a block, not inside for loops.]

62.reason: [Variable 'sign' is declared inside the for loop initializer block, which is not allowed in C89/C90.]
